title: Rakatan Civil War

{{Quote|Oaka Prime. I loved this place even before I knew it actually existed for real. So many children's holonovels were set in this mythical place. Way, way back during the Rakatan Empire, this world was designated as a wildlife sanctuary, away from the wars with the Gree and the Kwa. Then the slave revolts and a plague collapsed the Empire, and Oaka was forgotten.|[[Seren Song]], to [[R4-00]]|<ref name="SW20">[[Star Wars (2013) 20|''Star Wars'' (2013) 20]]</ref>}}
The '''Rakatan Civil War''' was a conflict that tore apart the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]]. Starting as internal strife, it was fueled by mass revolts of [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] [[Species/Legends|species]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. During the climax, a [[Rakatan plague|plague]] erupted that only infected the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]]. It caused the collapse of the Rakatan power and paved the way for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]].

==History==
{{Quote|Approximately 25,200 B.B.Y., a plague decimated the Rakatan species; the surviving Rakata devolved into barbarism and eventually forgot how to operate their own technology|[[Tionne Solusar]], ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]''|<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
Internal conflicts<ref name="TORE">''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]''</ref> and civil war<ref name="TUR">''[[The Unknown Regions]]''</ref> were the first cracks in the integrity of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]].<ref name="TORE" /> Around [[25,200 BBY]], a [[Rakatan plague|plague]] which affected only the [[Rakata/Legends|Rakata]] spread throughout the entirety of the Empire, killing billions of Rakata and an apparent mutation of the plague severed the survivors connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].<ref name="TNEC" /> The civil war and the plague weakened the Rakata's hold on the [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] populations.<ref name="TUR" /> Sensing the weakness in their Rakatan [[Predor|overlords]], [[Human/Legends|Humans]]<ref name="TORE" /> and well as [[Duros/Legends|Duros]], [[Herglic/Legends|Herglics]], [[Baragwin/Legends|Baragwins]], [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]], and [[Gossam/Legends|Gossams]], rose up against their<ref name="TEGtW" /> overlords in a series of massive violent slave revolts.<ref name="TORE" /> With the Rakata's Force [[Force-sensitive/Legends|connection]] severed, [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]] fell into civil war, even as the [[Star Forge]] hung in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], inoperable due to the Rakata's severed Force connection.<ref name="TNEC" /> The slaves where successful and regained control of their subjugated worlds from the Rakata.<ref name="KOTORCG">''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''</ref>

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|It is possible that to avenge themselves, their former subjects attempted to erase their oppressors from history, destroying all the technology they used to oppress them and leaving them in a primitive state. Left in dwindling numbers, they soon became extinct.|[[Mammon Hoole]], [[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species (book)|''The New Essential Guide to Alien Species'']]|<ref name="TNEGtAS">''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]''</ref>}}
In the aftermath of the plague and civil war, the Rakata devolved into barbarism and forgot how to operate their own technology.<ref name="JvS" /> The remaining Rakata were forced to retreat to their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], Lehon, and their once powerful forces reduced to squabbling [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] and feuding clans, both on Lehon and on isolated, off-world colonies.<ref name="KOTORCG" /> It is a possibility that the Rakata's former subjects tried to erase the Rakata from history.<ref name="TNEGtAS" /> With [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] now freed from Rakatan control, members of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] formed the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in [[25,053 BBY]].<ref name="TORE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The order of events varies between sources. [[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species (book)|''The New Essential Guide to Alien Species'']] states that the Rakatan Civil War started with a revolt of subjugated populations, and then ended with the plague.<ref name="TNEGtAS" /> Because other sources such as ''[[The Essential Atlas]]''<ref name="TEA" /> and ''[[The Unknown Regions]]''<ref name="TUR" /> describe the plague as the cause of the revolt, this article assumes the plague happened first.
